# drydock

> A sandbox for autonomous coding agents on macOS: per-task hardware-isolated
> VMs, a credential gateway (the agent never sees your real API key),
> deny-by-default egress, and a diff-only return path gated by human approval.
> Runs Claude Code, Codex, or any OpenAI-compatible model. Requires macOS 26+
> on Apple silicon. Apache-2.0.

- [Home](https://sricola.github.io/drydock/): what drydock is and how the isolation works

## Docs

- [drydock docs](https://sricola.github.io/drydock/docs/index.html): Operator documentation for drydock: a sandbox for running coding agents (Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, or any OpenAI-compatible model) on your own repos, on…
- [Quickstart](https://sricola.github.io/drydock/docs/quickstart.html): Install drydock and run your first sandboxed task in about a minute. The agent runs full-throttle in a throwaway VM; the only thing that comes back is a…
- [Authentication](https://sricola.github.io/drydock/docs/authentication.html): drydock runs Claude Code (Anthropic), OpenAI Codex (OpenAI), and Gemini (Google), each with a vendor API key or, for Claude and Codex, your existing…
- [Models](https://sricola.github.io/drydock/docs/models.html): drydock has a native gemini agent that speaks Google's own wire format: Google's auth header (x-goog-api-key) and a usageMetadata token-metering parser…
- [Submitting tasks](https://sricola.github.io/drydock/docs/submitting-tasks.html): A task is one unit of work: a repo, an instruction, and an agent. drydock clones the repo into a throwaway VM, runs the agent, captures a git diff, and…
- [Web UI](https://sricola.github.io/drydock/docs/web-ui.html): drydock ui serves a small web app over the broker socket: the same board, approval gate, diff, and history you get from the CLI, in a browser. It binds to…
- [Run unattended (launchd daemon)](https://sricola.github.io/drydock/docs/daemon.html): drydock daemon install runs brokerd as a launchd LaunchAgent: it starts at login, survives reboots, and restarts on crash. Tasks can be submitted while…
- [Egress & widening](https://sricola.github.io/drydock/docs/egress.html): The sandbox's internet access is deny-by-default. The agent can reach only the hosts on your allowlist; everything else is blocked. This is what stops a…
- [Configuration](https://sricola.github.io/drydock/docs/configuration.html): drydock init creates ~/.drydock/ (mode 0700) and seeds two files:
- [Troubleshooting](https://sricola.github.io/drydock/docs/troubleshooting.html): Start with drydock doctor; it smoke-tests the sandbox setup (image freshness, VM boot, the egress pin) with no API spend. Then consult the table below.
- [Threat model](https://sricola.github.io/drydock/docs/threat-model.html): drydock runs an LLM coding agent on hardware it doesn't trust the agent to operate. This document is precise about what that buys you and what it does…

## Source

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/sricola/drydock): source, releases, issue tracker
